recentFeedback.summary: How do I download files from the media panel
recentFeedback.text: How do I download files from the media panel? Someone shared a project with me and I want to download their source media to test with a different prototype but I can't figure out how to do it. Thanks in advance!
recentFeedback.parent_text: How do I download files from the media panel? Someone shared a project with me and I want to download their source media to test with a different prototype but I can't figure out how to do it. Thanks in advance!

recentFeedback.summary:
there is no user-facing feature for this

you can use <https://git
recentFeedback.text:
there is no user-facing feature for this

you can use <https://git.corp.adobe.com/Horizon/hz/blob/main/docs/copying-projects.md|toby> to download the whole project and media if you are so inclined

there is also a developer feature to download original media file (from the timeline). feature flag is `enable-asset-download`

eg add `?feature-enable=enable-asset-download` to the project URL and you should get a new right-click menu in timeline
recentFeedback.parent_text: How do I download files from the media panel? Someone shared a project with me and I want to download their source media to test with a different prototype but I can't figure out how to do it. Thanks in advance!

recentFeedback.text: This was more of a dev feature that didn't go thru the whole feature design etc etc so remained hidden. I don't know if there would be any objection to it (other than I think we'd want it in the media panel menu rather than TL)
recentFeedback.parent_text: How do I download files from the media panel? Someone shared a project with me and I want to download their source media to test with a different prototype but I can't figure out how to do it. Thanks in advance!

recentFeedback.summary:
I may not be fully understanding <@W4RRH6ZDY> ‘s ask, but…

You (and any user) can go into the project at <http://adobe
recentFeedback.text:
I may not be fully understanding <@W4RRH6ZDY> ‘s ask, but…

You (and any user) can go into the project at <http://adobe.com/files|adobe.com/files> and then download anything you want from the Media folder inside the project.

recentFeedback.summary:
When I open the cool Generate Media widget, a few accessibility observations:
I see the Video and Audio tabs
recentFeedback.text:
When I open the cool Generate Media widget, a few accessibility observations:
I see the Video and Audio tabs.  When I tab to Audio and select it with Enter using accessibility keyboard, I cannot shift+tab back to select Video.  I can select Video using left/right arrows.

I should be able to tab to VIDEO because the semantic HTML has not declared this a tab list in the HTML.  If this is meant to be a tablist, the semantic html needs to show role=tablist.
recentFeedback.parent_text:
When I open the cool Generate Media widget, a few accessibility observations:
I see the Video and Audio tabs.  When I tab to Audio and select it with Enter using accessibility keyboard, I cannot shift+tab back to select Video.  I can select Video using left/right arrows.

I should be able to tab to VIDEO because the semantic HTML has not declared this a tab list in the HTML.  If this is meant to be a tablist, the semantic html needs to show role=tablist.

recentFeedback.summary: If this is not a tablist I should be able to tab to VIDEO because the semantic HTML has not declared this a tab list in
recentFeedback.text:
If this is not a tablist I should be able to tab to VIDEO because the semantic HTML has not declared this a tab list in the HTML.

If this is meant to be a tablist, using the arrow keys is fine but the semantic html still needs to show:
-for tab list role="tablist".
-for tab role = "tab"
-for tab panel role = "tabpanel"

On selection of a tab, aria=selected=true.  Inactive tabs should display aria-selected=false.

The active tab has tabindex="0"
All other tabs must have tabindex="-1"

recentFeedback.summary: I didn't know this either, but apparently a tab list allows navigating via tabs using left/right arrow keys to navigate
recentFeedback.text:
I didn't know this either, but apparently a tab list allows navigating via tabs using left/right arrow keys to navigate the tabs.  Tab keyboarding should not allow the user to navigate out of the tab list using keyboard, but should not go to the next tab.

If the semantic html declares the role="tablist" and a tab list is issued, navigation to a different tab is not needed via tab.  Left/Right arrow keys are fine to navigate tabs.

If the semantic html does not declare role="tablist" then tab should be allowed meaning the user should be able to tab to SOUND select it, and then if s/he made a mistake press Shift+Tab to navigate back to Video and select it.

recentFeedback.summary: I circled back on this and the issue is with the way the semantic HTML is set up
recentFeedback.text:
I circled back on this and the issue is with the way the semantic HTML is set up.  <https://jira.corp.adobe.com/browse/DVAWV-20790|DVAWV-20790> can be closed to track out these two new issues.
```Issue 1:  When a user tabs from the tab into the tab panel, Screen Readers are not announcing the relationship because the panel is missing aria-labelled-by="<tab name>" and the tab is missing aria-controls="<tab panel>"  which shows the relationship between the tab and the panel to the Screen Reader.

Expected Result:
When a user opens Generate Media Panel and the user tabs from the Sound or Video tab into the tab panel, the screen reader should say:  "<Tab Name>, tab panel" OR "Tab name>, region" OR "Tab Panel labeled <Tab Name>" Instead, the Screen Reader announces nothing until the user tabs into the Generative Prompt Textbox.```
```Issue 2:  When a user opens the Generate Media Panel and then tabs to a tab and presses SPACE bar to select it, the video inadvertently plays causing an unintentional action.  The user meant to select the tab, but is also playing the Video possibly due to the focus and the event listener.

Expected Result:
When the user places focus on the tab and presses Space Bar to select the tab, it should not trigger two events and play the video.```

recentFeedback.summary:
thanks, robert.

re: issue 2... yeah, we have a few places where traditional keyboard shortcuts used to interact with co
recentFeedback.text:
thanks, robert.

re: issue 2... yeah, we have a few places where traditional keyboard shortcuts used to interact with controls conflict with traditional video editor shortcuts (i.e., Spacebar for play/stop, Left/Right Arrow for moving to previous/next frame or Up/Down Arrow for moving to previous/next edit point).
